elements.name: PATIENT NAME [1]
elements.definition: Patient’s name as per identification document.
elements.guidance:
• The element (Name) should be recorded as mentioned in the official Saudi proof of identity issued
by The Ministry of Interior.

• If the patient is non-Saudi and does not have the local proof of identity, then use passport
information

Example

• Mohammad Aziz Almajid
elements.validationRules: Name should be recorded as per the official proof of identity
elements.rawText:
DATA ELEMENT             DESCRIPTION

Definition               Patient’s name as per identification document.

Data Type                Alphabet

Format                   Text

Mandatory / Optional     Mandatory

Encounter Types          All Encounters

Code Set                 None

Guidance for Use

• The element (Name) should be recorded as mentioned in the official Saudi proof of identity issued
     by The Ministry of Interior.

• If the patient is non-Saudi and does not have the local proof of identity, then use passport
     information

Example

• Mohammad Aziz Almajid

Validation Rules         Name should be recorded as per the official proof of identity

Maximum Occurrences      1

elements.name: MEDICAL RECORD NUMBER (MRN) [1]
elements.definition:
The medical record number is organization specific.
The number is used by the hospital as a systematic documentation of
a patient’s medical history and care during each hospital stay or visit.
elements.guidance:
• This element provides a specified key to a specified record.
• This medical record number, is unique within a region of interest

Example

167192

Element Classification  Top Secret

elements.name: BLOOD GROUP [7][8]
elements.definition:
Types of blood as represented by codes – A, B, AB, and O. The blood
group is determined by the genes a person inherit from their parents.
Each group can be either RhD positive or RhD negative.
elements.guidance:
• Code 888888888 – refers to values or descriptions which are Not Found
• Code 999999999 – refers to Not Mapped values or descriptions
elements.validationRules: Codes should be selected from the provided list
elements.rawText:
DATA ELEMENT            DESCRIPTION

Definition              Types of blood as represented by codes – A, B, AB, and O. The blood
                        group is determined by the genes a person inherit from their parents.
                        Each group can be either RhD positive or RhD negative.

Data Type               Numeric

Format                  Number

Mandatory / Optional    Mandatory

Valid Encounter Types   All Encounters

Code set                ISO200 SeHE – SNOMED CT

Code                    Short Description  Long Description

278147001               O+                 Blood group O Rh(D) positive
278148006               O–                 Blood group O Rh(D) negative
278149003               A+                 Blood group A Rh(D) positive
278150003               B+                 Blood group B Rh(D) positive
278151004               AB +               Blood group AB Rh(D) positive
278152006               A–                 Blood group A Rh(D) negative
278153001               B–                 Blood group B Rh(D) negative
278154007               AB –               Blood group AB Rh(D) negative
888888888               Others             Others
999999999               Unknown            Unknown

Guidance for Use

• Code 888888888 – refers to values or descriptions which are Not Found
• Code 999999999 – refers to Not Mapped values or descriptions

Validation Rules        Codes should be selected from the provided list

Maximum Occurrences     1
Element Classification  Public

elements.name: GENDER [1]
elements.definition: Sex of the patient
elements.codeSet:
Code                    Description

1                       Male

2                       Female

3                       Undifferentiated

88                      Others

99                      Unknown
elements.guidance:
• Code 3 will be used when handling a newborn or a fetus that is not yet decided for whatever
reasons not to be used for reporting purposes when data is not completed.

• Code 88 is used for the cases where no data is available on the patient sex and can never be
retrieved.

• Code 99 is used under those physical condition that does not allow sex recognition or sex is not
mapped.
elements.rawText:
DATA ELEMENT            DESCRIPTION

Definition              Sex of the patient

Data Type               Numeric

Format                  N

Mandatory / Optional    Mandatory

Encounter Types         All Encounters

Code Set

Code                    Description

1                       Male

2                       Female

3                       Undifferentiated

88                      Others

99                      Unknown

Guidance for Use

• Code 3 will be used when handling a newborn or a fetus that is not yet decided for whatever
     reasons not to be used for reporting purposes when data is not completed.

• Code 88 is used for the cases where no data is available on the patient sex and can never be
     retrieved.

• Code 99 is used under those physical condition that does not allow sex recognition or sex is not
     mapped.

   Validation Rules     Field cannot be empty

   Maximum Occurrences  1

Element Classification       Public

elements.name: TRIAGE LEVEL [1]
elements.definition:
The category assigned to a patient based of an initial assessment by
medical or nursing staff in an Accident and Emergency Department.
The triage category is used to determine the Patient’s priority for
treatment, and to inform the patient of their waiting time.
elements.codeSet:
Code                    Description

1                       Level 1: Resuscitation

2                       Level 2: Emergent

3                       Level 3: Urgent

4                       Level 4: Less urgent

5                       Level 5: Non-urgent

88                      Others

99                      Unknown
elements.guidance:
• Level 1: Resuscitation – Conditions that are threats to life or limb
• Level 2: Emergent – Conditions that are a potential threat to life, limb or function
• Level 3: Urgent – Serious conditions that require emergency intervention
• Level 4: Less urgent – Conditions that relate to patient distress or potential complications that

would benefit from intervention
• Level 5: Non-urgent – Conditions that are non-urgent or that may be part of a chronic problem
• Code 88 refers to values or descriptions which are Not Found
• Code 99 refers to Not Mapped values or descriptions

elements.name: ED DISCHARGE DISPOSITION [1][2]
elements.definition:
The discharge status of the patient at the end of the non-admitted
patient emergency department service episode represented by a
code
elements.codeSet:
Code                    Description

1                       Admitted

2                       Discharged

3                       Transferred

4                       LWBS / Absconding

5                       DAMA

6                       Deceased

7                       Dead on Arrival

88                      Others

99                      Unknown
elements.guidance:
• Admitted – Admitted to Inpatient treating facility
• Discharged – Patient discharged home without being admitted or referred to another hospital.
• Transferred –Patient transferred to another hospital for admission.
• Deceased – Died in ED
• DAMA – Patient choosing to leave the hospital before the treating physician recommends

discharge.
• LWBS / Absconding – Did not wait to be attended by a health care professional or can be referred
• Code 88 – Others – refers to values or descriptions which are Not Found
• Code 99 -refers to Not Mapped values or descriptions

elements.name: DISCHARGE DISPOSITION [1][2]
elements.definition:
This field contains a code which defines the circumstances under
which a patient discharged from the hospital. For majority of the
patients this is when they are discharged by the treating Physician
elements.codeSet:
Code                    Description

1                       Discharge

2                       Left against medical advice (LAMA)

3                       Discharge against medical advice (DAMA)

4                       Deceased

5                       Referred

6                       Discharge / Transfer to other hospital / facility

88                      Others

99                      Unknown
elements.guidance:
• Discharge – Implies that patient is discharged by treating physician
• LAMA – Implies that Patient leaves against medical advice without signing necessary medical

forms
• DAMA – It refers to patient who leave against medical advice after signing necessary medical form
• Deceased – It refers to those patients who died
• Referred- referred is the transfer of care for a patient from one healthcare setting to another by

request
• Discharge / Transfer to other accommodation – Patient is discharged or transferred by treating

physician to another healthcare facility
• Code 88 – Others – refers to values or descriptions which are Not Found
• Code 99 – Unknown – refers to Not Mapped values or descriptions

codeTable.description: Discharge / Transfer to other hospital / facility

elements.rawText:

Guidance for Use

• Discharge – Implies that patient is discharged by treating physician
• LAMA – Implies that Patient leaves against medical advice without signing necessary medical

     forms
• DAMA – It refers to patient who leave against medical advice after signing necessary medical form
• Deceased – It refers to those patients who died
• Referred- referred is the transfer of care for a patient from one healthcare setting to another by

     request
• Discharge / Transfer to other accommodation – Patient is discharged or transferred by treating

     physician to another healthcare facility
• Code 88 – Others – refers to values or descriptions which are Not Found
• Code 99 – Unknown – refers to Not Mapped values or descriptions

condition mandates such assessment

• The reading is entered as a value for the examination

elements.name: BED STATUS
elements.definition: This field describes the occupancy status of the hospital bed.
elements.codeSet:
Code                    Description

1                       Vacant

2                       Cleaning

3                       Occupied

4                       Temporary Closed

88                      Others

99                      Unknown
elements.guidance:
• Vacant Bed – Beds that are vacant, licensed, physically available and staffed and to which patients
can be admitted immediately.

• Cleaning Bed – Beds which are under maintenance or housekeeping and not ready for use.
• Occupied Bed – Beds that are licensed, physically available, staffed and occupied by a patient.
• Temporary Closed – Beds which would qualify as available in all other respects but are temporarily

unavailable due to redecoration, quarantine or lack of staff and will be back in use within a very
short period, not more than one month, from the time they were closed.
• Code 88 – refers to values or descriptions which are Not Found
• Code 99 – refers to Not Mapped values or descriptions

elements.name: ISOLATION ROOM STATUS
elements.definition: A Room status classification based on respiratory isolation
elements.codeSet:
Code                    Description

1                       Isolation Normal Pressure (NP)

2                       Isolation Negative Pressure (-VEP)

3                       Isolation Positive Pressure (+VEP)

4                       Non-Isolation

88                      Others

99                      Unknown
elements.guidance:
• Isolation Normal Pressure – Room status which aims to control the airflow so that the number of
airborne infectious particles is reduced to a level that ensures cross-infection of other people
within a healthcare facility is highly unlikely

• Isolation Negative Pressure – Rooms with quantity of air pressure lower than the normal air
pressure

• Isolation Positive Pressure – Rooms with quantity of air pressure greater than the normal air
pressure

• Non-Isolation – These are standard pressure rooms with normal air pressure and air conditioning
• Code 88 – refers to values or descriptions which are Not Found
• Code 99 –refers to Not Mapped values or descriptions

Example
• Patients with respiratory / infectious / communicable diseases requires Isolation room
• Isolation Negative Pressure rooms are needed for patients who require airborne droplet nuclei

isolation like pathogens such as measles, varicella zoster (chicken pox), legionella, tuberculosis
• Isolation Positive Pressure rooms are needed to isolate immune compromised patients, for

example oncology and some transplant patients. The intent is to reduce the risk of airborne
transmission of infection to a susceptible patient

Element Classification  Public

elements.name: LAB ORDER PRIORITY [8]
elements.definition:
Denotes values which allows the clinical professionals to categorize
and order Lab tests based upon the medical urgency during the
current encounter
elements.codeSet:
Code                    Description

1                       STAT

2                       Urgent

3                       Routine

88                      Others

99                      Unknown
elements.guidance:
• STAT – refers to immediately lab orders without delay
• Urgent – A priority lower than STAT
• Routine – within 48 hours of order
• Code 88 – refers to values or descriptions which are Not Found
• Code 99 – refers to Not Mapped values or descriptions

elements.guidance:

• Urgent – A priority request that is lower than emergency but takes precedence over ordinary

priority
• Elective – Non-Emergent and can be delayed
• Code 88 – Others – refers to values or descriptions which are Not Found
• Code 99 – Unknown – refers to Not Mapped values or descriptions

elements.name: REFERRAL PRIORITY [8]
elements.definition:
It describes the priority of service/treatment request from the referral
hospital / PHC to the current hospital for appropriate surgical
procedure
elements.codeSet:
Code                    Description

1                       Emergency

2                       Urgent

3                       Elective

88                      Others

99                      Unknown
elements.guidance:
• Emergency – Priority without delay
• Urgent – A priority request that is lower than emergency but takes precedence over ordinary

priority
• Elective – Non-Emergent and can be delayed
• Code 88 – Others – refers to values or descriptions which are Not Found
• Code 99 – Unknown – refers to Not Mapped values or descriptions

subcutaneous tissues

elements.name: LACERATIONS
elements.definition:
A laceration is a tear in the tissue or muscle around patient vagina
and perineum during labor. Grades of lacerations are determined by
the severity of the tear.
elements.codeSet:
Code                    Description

1                       First Degree Tear

2                       Second Degree Tear

3                       Third-Degree Tear

4                       Fourth-Degree Tear

88                      Others

99                      Unknown
elements.guidance:
• First Degree Tear - This is the least severe and involves a small tear of the perineal skin.
• Second Degree Tear - This injury is the most common and is a slightly deeper tear that extends to

the skin and muscle of the perineum and sometimes the vagina.
• Third-Degree Tear - This level can tear into the muscles from the vagina to the anus
• Fourth-Degree Tear - This injury can extend from the vagina to the anal sphincter muscles and

into the rectum

elements.name: BIRTH OUTCOME [1]
elements.definition: A code used to indicate whether a birth is a live, stillbirth.
elements.codeSet:
Code                    Description

1                       Live

2                       Stillbirth

88                      Others

99                      Unknown
elements.guidance:
• Live Birth: A live birth occurs when a fetus, whatever its gestational age, exits the maternal body
and subsequently shows any sign of life, such as voluntary movement, heartbeat, or pulsation of
the umbilical cord, for however brief a time.

• Still Birth: A stillbirth is the death or loss of a baby before or during delivery.

mapped.

• DAMA – It refers to patient who are discharged against medical advice after signing necessary

medical form
• Home/Residence – Patient discharge to home or residence
• Discharge / Transfer to other accommodation – Patient is discharged or transferred by treating

physician to another healthcare facility
• Deceased – It refers to those patients who died

elements.name: CAUSE OF DEATH (ICD 10 AM CODE) [1]
elements.definition:
The Cause of death to be entered on the death certificate of cause of
death are all those diseases, morbid conditions or injuries which
either resulted in or contributed to death and the circumstances of
the accident or violence which produced any such injuries. (ICD-10-
AM).
elements.codeSet: Codes used are ICD-10 AM diagnosis code
elements.guidance:
Underlying cause of death:
• The disease or injury which initiated the train of events leading directly to death.
• The circumstances of the accident or violence which produced the fatal injury
• Up to 5 causes of death may be submitted

Contributory causes of death:
• Diabetes in the case of myocardial infarction
• Malnutrition in the case of measles
• Alcohol intake in the case of motor vehicle accident

• In case of multiple entries, provide in separate rows

elements.name: TOOTH SURFACE [14]
elements.definition:
Tooth surfaces are labelled according to their relation to the midline
or to structures in the oral cavity as represented by a code
elements.codeSet:
Code                  Description

1                     Mesial (M)

2                     Distal (D)

3                     Labial (L)

4                     Buccal (B)

5                     Lingual

6                     Palatal

7                     Occlusal (O)

8                     Incisal (I)

9                     Proximal (interproximal, approximal)

10                    Facial (F)

11                    Cervical

12                    Apical

88                    Others

99                    Unknown
elements.guidance:
• Mesial (M) - Nearest to the midline of the dental arch
• Distal (D) - Furthest from the midline of the dental arch
• Labial (L) - Next to the lips (incisors and canines)
• Buccal (B) - Next to the cheeks (molars and premolars)
• Lingual - Next to the tongue (mandibular teeth only)
• Palatal - Next to the palate (maxillary teeth only)
• Occlusal (O) - The biting surface of posterior teeth (molars and premolars)
• Incisal (I) - The cutting edge of anterior teeth (incisors and canines)
• Proximal (interproximal, approximal) - Mesial and distal surfaces
• Facial (F) - Buccal and labial surfaces
• Cervical - The junction between the crown and root
• Apical - At the apex of the tooth root
• Code 88 – refers to values or descriptions which are Not Found
• Code 99 - refers to Not Mapped values or descriptions

• Mesial (M) - Nearest to the midline of the dental arch
• Distal (D) - Furthest from the midline of the dental arch
• Labial (L) - Next to the lips (incisors and canines)
• Buccal (B) - Next to the cheeks (molars and premolars)
• Lingual - Next to the tongue (mandibular teeth only)
• Palatal - Next to the palate (maxillary teeth only)
• Occlusal (O) - The biting surface of posterior teeth (molars and premolars)
• Incisal (I) - The cutting edge of anterior teeth (incisors and canines)
• Proximal (interproximal, approximal) - Mesial and distal surfaces
• Facial (F) - Buccal and labial surfaces
• Cervical - The junction between the crown and root
• Apical - At the apex of the tooth root

Element Classification  Public

elements.name: TOOTH PART [15]
elements.definition: Parts of teeth as represented by a code
elements.codeSet:
Code                  Description

1                     Enamel

2                     Anatomical Crown

3                     Gums / Gingiva

4                     Pulp Chamber

5                     Neck

6                     Dentin

7                     Jawbone (Alveolar Bone)

8                     Root Canal

9                     Cementum

10                    Periodontal Ligament

88                    Others

99                    Unknown
elements.guidance:
• Enamel - Hard calcified tissue covering the dentin in the crown of tooth
• Anatomical Crown - The visible part of tooth covered by enamel
• Gums / Gingiva - Soft tissues that cover and protect the roots of your teeth and cover teeth that

have not yet erupted
• Pulp Chamber - The space occupied by the soft tissue at the center of teeth containing nerves,

blood vessels and connective tissue
• Neck - The area where the crown joins the root
• Dentin - That part of the tooth that is beneath enamel and cementum
• Jawbone (Alveolar Bone) - The part of the jaw that surrounds the roots of the teeth
• Root Canal - The portion of the pulp cavity inside the root of a tooth
• Cementum - Hard connective tissue covering the tooth root, giving attachment to the periodontal

ligament
• Periodontal Ligament - A system of collagenous connective tissue fibers that connect the root of a

tooth to its socket.
• Code 88 – refers to values or descriptions which are Not Found
• Code 99 - refers to Not Mapped values or descriptions

elements.name: FACILITY TYPE [1]
elements.definition: A code that categorizes facilities into particular types
elements.guidance:
• Allied Medical Service – This refers to non-hospital based allied medical services like Radiology
Centers, Medical Laboratories, Optician, Pharmacies

• General Hospital - Facility that can handle most people and does not limit itself to a specified type
of medical specialty or a higher level of medical care

• Specialist Hospitals - Facilities that has more than 300 operational hospital beds and recruits
excluding Eye Hospitals

• Medical City – Facility with more than one hospital under direct operation and physically in one
location regardless of the type of service provided whether general care or specialized care

• Field Hospital - Describe a mobile health facility that provides full hospitalization services that are
used in disaster management or abnormal situations

• Allied Medical Service – This refers to non-hospital based allied medical services like Radiology

• General Hospital - Facility that can handle most people and does not limit itself to a specified type
     of medical specialty or a higher level of medical care

• Specialist Hospitals - Facilities that has more than 300 operational hospital beds and recruits
     excluding Eye Hospitals

• Medical City – Facility with more than one hospital under direct operation and physically in one
     location regardless of the type of service provided whether general care or specialized care

• Field Hospital - Describe a mobile health facility that provides full hospitalization services that are
     used in disaster management or abnormal situations

elements.definition: Sex of the patient
elements.codeSet:
Code                    Description

1                       Male

2                       Female

3                       Undifferentiated

88                      Others

99                      Unknown
elements.guidance:
• Code 3 will be used when handling a newborn or a fetus that is not yet decided for whatever
reasons not to be used for reporting purposes when data is not completed.

• Code 88 is used for the cases where no data is available on the patient sex and can never be
retrieved.

• Code 99 is used under those physical condition that does not allow sex recognition or sex is not
mapped.

Element Classification  Public

elements.name: REFERRED BY FACILITY TYPE
elements.definition: A code that categorizes facilities into particular types
elements.codeSet:
Code                    Description

A1                      PHC, Clinics, Polyclinics, Prehospital EMS units, Mobile Clinics

A2                      Allied Medical Service

H1                      General Hospital

H2                      Specialist Hospital

H3                      Medical City

H4                      Field Hospital

H5                      Secondary

H6                      Tertiary

H7                      Normal PHC

H8                      Mega PHC
elements.guidance:
• Allied Medical Service – This refers to non-hospital based allied medical services like Radiology
Centers, Medical Laboratories, Optician, Pharmacies

• General Hospital - Facility that can handle most people and does not limit itself to a specified type
of medical specialty or a higher level of medical care

• Specialist Hospitals - Facilities that has more than 300 operational hospital beds and recruits
excluding Eye Hospitals

• Medical City – Facility with more than one hospital under direct operation and physically in one
location regardless of the type of service provided whether general care or specialized care

• Field Hospital - Describe a mobile health facility that provides full hospitalization services that are
used in disaster management or abnormal situations

codeTable.description: PHC, Clinics, Polyclinics, Prehospital EMS units, Mobile Clinics
